item.title: FirstFT: UK crime agency reviewed Mandelson allegations in 2024
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: US Navy secretary fired and EU Arctic drilling rethink

item.title: Japan moves to block foreign takeover deal on national security grounds
item.description: Government asks South Korean private equity group MBK Partners to drop attempt to buy Makino Milling

item.title: Escape from Hormuz: the oil tankers running the Iranian gauntlet
item.description: How trading houses Vitol, Trafigura and Mercuria have managed to get some of their oil tankers out of the Gulf

item.title: PE firm L Catterton launches new fund staked by top athletes
item.description: LVMH-backed group will seek to harness influence of sports personalities to enhance portfolio companies

item.title: Private equity courts OpenAI and Anthropic
item.description: The start-ups are in talks with private equity firms over joint ventures intended to help deploy AI in businesses

item.title: Nelson Peltz’s son builds first public activist stake in Intertek
item.description: Matt Peltz’s new firm Lost Coast Collective has invested in the UK testing company

item.title: Quant pioneer Martin Lueck warns against handing over trading to AI
item.description: Caution by co-founder of Aspect hedge fund follows billionaire Cliff Asness’s decision to ‘surrender’ to the machines

item.title: House of Fidelity — the dynasty at the heart of the investment powerhouse
item.description: A journalist lifts the lid on how the Johnson family battled to keep control of the asset manager in a history packed with luminaries of finance

item.title: UK could save £2.5bn by helping banks to buy gilts, says Barclays
item.description: Idea long pursued by banking sector lobbyists faces opposition from regulators

item.title: Top Republican pushes party to shun $300mn AI lobby
item.description: Senator Josh Hawley warns of ‘political cost’ if Washington fails to rein in Big Tech and artificial intelligence

item.title: High earners race ahead on AI as workplace divide widens
item.description: FT-Focaldata poll offers early evidence on how the technology is reshaping work and reinforcing gender and pay gaps

item.title: Will energy security fears change the global energy market? With Daniel Yergin
item.description: What the Iran conflict means for oil stockpiles, gas and renewables

item.title: The AI digital divide
item.description: An FT survey shows the highest-earning workers are adopting the technology in their jobs far faster than others

item.title: SpaceX and Tesla are on an inevitable collision course
item.description: To the extent shareholders just want exposure to Elon Musk, a single security seems like the sensible end state

item.title: Nvidia supplier SK Hynix hails ‘structural shift’ after another record quarter
item.description: Second-largest memory chipmaker says customers prioritising procurement over pricing amid supply crunch

item.title: Partitions and permit delays: the UK law firms sticking with China
item.description: Regulatory scrutiny over operations — down to office seating plans — is price to pay for firms wanting to do business in the country

item.title: Intel boosted as Musk says his Terafab will use its latest chipmaking tech
item.description: US semiconductor group has been seeking a big customer for its ‘14A’ manufacturing system

item.title: Argentina’s economy records sharpest monthly contraction under Milei
item.description: Manufacturing and retail sectors post sharp declines in economic activity

item.title: Boeing regains delivery lead over Airbus as revenues jump
item.description: US aircraft maker narrows losses and posts widest gap over European rival since 2018

item.title: Iran war drives Panama Canal lane prices to record high
item.description: Asian buyers seeking western crude push bids five times greater than pre-conflict levels

item.title: Isa confusion clouds UK’s launch of retail investing campaign
item.description: ‘Savvy the Squirrel’ initiative comes as investors await clarity on eligibility of products in stocks-and-shares products

item.title: Middle East crisis as it happened: Iran says it has seized two container ships in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Oil rises back above $100 a barrel following incidents in waters close to Iran

item.title: Investors nurse losses after ‘short squeeze’ at car rental group Avis Budget
item.description: US company’s equities had soared more than 400% in echo of Wall Street’s 2021 ‘meme stock’ craze

item.title: Tesla shares rally as profits rebound from last year’s lows
item.description: Net income rises 17% but is still second-weakest figure in five years

item.title: Builder.ai founder Sachin Dev Duggal accused of receiving siphoned funds
item.description: Indian authorities name UK start-up founder in criminal complaint over ties to collapsed electronics group

item.title: Crypto billionaire Justin Sun sues Trump family’s World Liberty Financial
item.description: Fraud claims come amid escalating feud between the Trump venture and one of its biggest supporters

item.title: Chelsea’s US owners sack another head coach
item.description: Liam Rosenior serves just three months of a five-and-a-half-year contract in London club’s latest managerial reshuffle

item.title: CVC-led group closes in on £1bn Standard Life pensions deal
item.description: Consortium, which includes Prudential Financial, would help fund buyouts of defined-benefit schemes

item.title: EU rethinks opposition to Arctic oil and gas drilling
item.description: U-turn by Brussels would be bitterly contested by environmental groups

item.title: Drahi’s €20bn SFR deal is a masterclass in hardball negotiation
item.description: Rivals may prefer to just watch him get into ever hotter water but buying his company outright gives them more certainty
